concerns.text: The plan cites 'Planck Legacy Archive (PR4)' as the source for CMB maps. As of the current scientific consensus and public release cycles, the Planck mission's final public data release is PR3 (2018). PR4 is not a publicly available, verified dataset with accessible URLs for programmatic download. Citing a non-existent or unreleased dataset version (PR4) renders the data acquisition step impossible to execute as written. The plan must cite the actual available release (PR3) or a specific, verified alternative source.

The plan assumes a 256³ particle N-body simulation in a 500 Mpc/h box will complete within 12 hours on a 2-core, 7GB RAM GitHub Actions runner. Standard N-body codes (GADGET-2) or Python wrappers (nbodykit) for this resolution typically require significantly more memory (often >16GB for full snapshot I/O and force calculation) and CPU time (often >24-48 hours for 256³ on 2 cores) to reach z=0 with sufficient accuracy. This compute assumption is infeasible for the stated free-tier constraints, risking immediate job termination or memory errors before the analysis can run.
